qa_pairs.question: Who should I contact for more information about the Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) process?
qa_pairs.answer: For more information about the RAC process, please contact allocations@tech.alliancecan.ca.

qa_pairs.question: Why is it necessary to allocate resources in advanced research computing?
qa_pairs.answer: Resource allocation is necessary because the advanced research computing (ARC) needs of the Canadian research community continue to grow, as next-generation scientific instruments are deployed, ARC becomes relevant to more disciplines, new datasets are gathered and mined, and technological advances allow for more precise models. Demand for resources has grown each year, and systems are currently running at maximum capacity.

qa_pairs.question: What factors are driving the increased demand for advanced research computing resources in Canada?
qa_pairs.answer: The increased demand is driven by the deployment of next-generation scientific instruments, the broadening relevance of advanced research computing (ARC) to various disciplines, the gathering and mining of new datasets in innovative ways, and technological advances that enable researchers to construct more precise models.

qa_pairs.question: Who is eligible to submit a Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) application?
qa_pairs.answer: To be eligible to submit a RAC application, Principal Investigators (PIs) and Co-PIs must be faculty members at a Canadian academic institution and have an active Alliance account with an Academic Principal Investigator role (Faculty, Adjunct Faculty, or Librarian).

qa_pairs.question: What are the specific requirements for the Alliance account role for RAC applicants?
qa_pairs.answer: Principal Investigators and Co-Principal Investigators must have an active Alliance account with an Academic Principal Investigator role, specifically Faculty, Adjunct Faculty, or Librarian.

qa_pairs.question: What should I do if my faculty position on the CCDB is outdated or not set as my primary role?
qa_pairs.answer: You must apply with your primary, most up-to-date position. If you recently moved institutions and haven't applied for a new faculty role on the CCDB, do so before submitting your RAC application. If you have multiple active faculty roles, ensure your most up-to-date position is set as your primary role by checking your Home page on the CCDB and clicking 'Make this role primary' if needed.

qa_pairs.question: What could be the consequences if I fail to update my primary position on the CCDB before submitting my RAC application?
qa_pairs.answer: Failing to update your primary position on the CCDB could create problems if your application is successful.

qa_pairs.question: Who can I contact if I have questions or concerns about the eligibility requirements for the RAC application?
qa_pairs.answer: If you have questions or concerns about any of the eligibility requirements, you can contact allocations@tech.alliancecan.ca.

qa_pairs.question: What is the Resource Allocation Competition (RAC)?
qa_pairs.answer: The RAC is an annual process involving two review stages: a peer-review by over 90 discipline-specific experts from Canadian academic institutions who assess computational research projects and provide a score, and a technical review by Federation staff who verify resource accuracy and recommend national system allocations based on technical requirements.

qa_pairs.question: What are the two review processes involved in the RAC each year?
qa_pairs.answer: The RAC involves a peer-review process and a technical review process each year.

qa_pairs.question: What is the role of the peer-review process in the Resource Allocation Competition?
qa_pairs.answer: The peer-review process involves more than 90 discipline-specific experts from Canadian academic institutions who assess and rate the merits of submitted computational research projects, resulting in a single score that guides allocation decisions.

qa_pairs.question: What is the purpose of the technical review within the RAC process?
qa_pairs.answer: The technical review is undertaken by Federation staff to verify the accuracy of the computational resources needed for each project based on the application's technical requirements and to make recommendations about which national system should be allocated to meet the project's needs.

qa_pairs.question: Who oversees the overall Resource Allocation Competition process?
qa_pairs.answer: The overall RAC process is overseen by the Resource Access Program Administrative Committee, which includes representatives from each of the national system host sites.

qa_pairs.question: Does the Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) provide any funding?
qa_pairs.answer: No, the RAC does not provide funding. It offers access to computing time, storage space, and cloud resources in their systems.

qa_pairs.question: What types of resources are offered through the RAC?
qa_pairs.answer: Through the RAC, access to computing time, storage space, and cloud resources in their systems is offered.

qa_pairs.question: What is the Fast Track process in the context of the RAC?
qa_pairs.answer: The Fast Track process is an application mechanism that gives eligible Principal Investigators access to an easy and lightweight multi-year application for the Resources for Research Group (RRG) competition. It is designed for researchers who wish to maintain their existing allocation with minimal changes to their scientific approach or resource needs.

qa_pairs.question: Who is invited to apply through the Fast Track process?
qa_pairs.answer: Only those PIs who receive an invitation will be able to apply through the Fast Track process.

qa_pairs.question: Does applying through the Fast Track process guarantee the same resource allocation as the previous year?
qa_pairs.answer: No, applying through the Fast Track process does not guarantee you will receive the same allocation as last year. Fast Track requests are subject to resource scaling, which could lead to an increase or decrease in allocated resources compared to the previous year.

qa_pairs.question: Why was the Fast Track process created?
qa_pairs.answer: The Fast Track process was created to reduce the need to re-evaluate proposals from researchers and small groups who wish to maintain their existing allocation with no or minimal changes to their scientific approach or resource needs.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find more details about eligibility and other information regarding the Fast Track process?
qa_pairs.answer: To know more about eligibility for this process and other details about the Fast Track process, visit the RAC Fast Track Application Guide page.

qa_pairs.question: What types of resources are available for researchers to access through this competition?
qa_pairs.answer: Through this call for proposals, researchers will be able to request access to general-purpose systems, large shared memory systems, general-purpose computing on graphics processing units, storage, and cloud resources.

qa_pairs.question: Does the competition include technical support and training for researchers?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, a broad spectrum of advanced computing services in support of research is provided, including technical support and training.
